methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 25 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 42.9x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Open Cosmos, a Growth-stage space and aerospace company, has raised $50M to date across its founding team of Rafel Jorda Siquier, Jordi Barrera Ars, and Aleix Megias Homar. The broader sector comprises 5 public comps (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, and Blue Origin, though direct peer valuation data at Open Cosmos's stage and scale proved limited.

Given the data constraints, only one valuation method was applicable: stage-progression analysis. Using the $50M total raised as a proxy for current valuation and applying a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of Growth-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $60M. Public-comp anchoring could not proceed—while the Space & Aerospace sector median trades at 42.9x P/S across six public names, Open Cosmos's revenue is not on file, precluding a revenue-multiple approach. Peer-median triangulation was similarly unworkable; despite 25 same-sector peers existing in databases, fewer than three comparable Growth-stage or adjacent-stage companies met filtering thresholds, rendering sectoral medians unrepresentative.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $51M to $69M, centered on $60M. This range reflects modest upside from capital raised but is constrained by the absence of direct comparables at identical stage and scale. Key uncertainties include Open Cosmos's actual revenue run rate (critical for P/S validation against public comps) and the availability of more granular private transaction data in micro-satellite or space-tech segments. The single-method foundation warrants marked conservatism in weighting this range.
